description: The Mining Remediation Authority makes a better future for people and the environment in mining areas. Mining Remediation Authority is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero .

details.body:
The Mining Remediation Authority makes a better future for people and the environment in mining areas.

Mining Remediation Authority is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Ochre from mine water treatment is being used to stabilise contaminated soil at a former lead works in Chester, making way for 126 new homes.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Mine water by-product unlocks city-centre brownfield site

ordered_featured_documents.summary: UK’s first mine water heat Living Lab is attracting global interest, with US and European researchers exploring its low-carbon heat data.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Global interest at mine heat living lab one-year milestone

image.alt_text: Carl Banton, Operations Director at the Mining Remediation Authority with Chris Deadman, Managing Director at Alpheus

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Mining Remediation Authority awards new operation and maintenance contract to Alpheus Environmental Limited.
ordered_featured_documents.title: New operation and maintenance contract awarded to Alpheus

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Carys Mills joins Mining Remediation Authority as part of programme to develop aspiring board members and boost diversity in public boardrooms.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Our third Boardroom Apprentice enjoys career-defining opportunity

ordered_featured_links.title: Access coal mining information and data

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information.

mainstream_browse_pages.description: Safety, permits, environmental impact and legacy issues
mainstream_browse_pages.title: Mining, quarrying and extraction

details.body: The chair is responsible for leading the board as they challenge and develop the Mining Remediation Authority’s strategy.

The Chief Finance and Information Officer is responsible for:

  • strategic leadership
  • financial management
  • development of the organisation’s information assets
  • ICT
  • business planning and legal functions

role_appointments.title: Paul Frammingham - Chief Finance and Information Officer

details.body: This role provides strategic leadership to the organisation and is accountable to Parliament and other stakeholders for the authority’s performance, ensuring its functions are carried out with efficiency, transparency and value for public money.

role.title: Interim Chief Executive and Accounting Officer

role_appointments.title: Paul Frammingham - Interim Chief Executive and Accounting Officer

details.body:
The Operations Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the organisation and its operational teams
  • building and maintaining strong relationships with key partners across England, Scotland and Wales to enable effective delivery of the organisation’s work
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence programme
  • leadership and delivery of the organisation’s functions, which include licensing coal mining operations, permitting work that impacts coal and coal mine workings, planning statutory consultation and managing organisation’s property
  • leadership and delivery of the coal tip safety work in Wales and Mining Remediation Authority owned tips

The role is responsible for:

  • Strategic direction and leadership across all the Mining Remediation Authority’s operational work and 24/7 frontline services
  • Statutory Director and Executive Board Member
  • Senior lead for all the Mining Remediation Authority’s work in Wales

role_appointments.title: Carl Banton - Interim Chief Operations Director

details.body:
The Operations and Sustainability Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the Coal Authority and its operational teams
  • building and maintaining strong relationships with key partners across England, Scotland and Wales to enable effective delivery of the Coal Authority’s work.
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence programme.
  • leadership and delivery of the Coal Authority Development functions which include licensing coal mining operations, permitting work that impacts coal and coal mine workings, planning statutory consultation and managing Coal Authority property.
  • leadership and delivery of the coal tip safety work in Wales and Coal Authority owned tips.
  • leadership and delivery of all aspects of the Coal Authority’s sustainability plan

role_appointments.title: Carl Banton - Operations and Sustainability Director

details.body:
The Operations Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the Coal Authority and its operational teams
  • leadership and delivery of the environment team’s mine water treatment programme
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence management programme
  • leadership of the safety, health and environmental matters
  • leadership of the Coal Authority’s team of engineers

The People and Resources Director is responsible for:

  • People and Organisational Development
  • Health Safety and Wellbeing
  • Portfolio Office
  • Procurement

role_appointments.title: James Lowth - People and Resources Director

details.body:
The Programme and Contract Assurance Director is responsible for:

  • contract assurance
  • programme office
  • engineering
  • procurement

role_appointments.title: James Lowth - Programme and Contract Assurance Director

details.body:
The Operations Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the Coal Authority and its operational teams
  • leadership and delivery of the environment team’s mine water treatment programme
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence management programme
  • leadership of the safety, health and environmental matters
  • leadership of the Coal Authority’s team of engineers

The Innovation and Engagement Director is responsible for:

  • Strategic development of all revenue generating reports, products and services
  • Sourcing and contract/supplier management
  • Strategic leadership of Innovation and Efficiency projects
  • Business Intelligence
  • Customer services

role_appointments.title: Richard Bond - Innovation and Engagement Director

details.body:
The Innovation and Services Director is responsible for:

  • leading the adoption of mine water heat as a low carbon heat source across the coalfields of Great Britain
  • strategic development of all value generating reports, products and advisory services
  • strategic leadership of innovation and heat projects
  • customer strategy and insights

role_appointments.title: Richard Bond - Innovation and Services Director

details.body:
This role is responsible for:

  • people and organisational development
  • health, safety, wellbeing and facilities
  • community response and stakeholder management
  • corporate and colleague engagement
  • CEO office

role_appointments.title: Victoria Slingsby - People and Engagement Director

details.body:
This role is responsible for:

  • leadership of the inland salinity programme
  • providing strategic input in to the Mining Remediation Authority’s executive leadership team, supporting the organisation’s delivery in line with its values

This role is responsible for:

  • environment operations
  • environment delivery – coal
  • environment delivery – metals

This role is responsible for:

  • digital - including service and assurance, technical delivery, web and design, architecture
  • information and artificial intelligence - including data management, data improvement, geospatial technologies and mining information consultants

details.body: The chair is responsible for leading the board as they challenge and develop the Mining Remediation Authority’s strategy.
The Chief Executive and Accounting Officer is responsible for how the authority spends public and departmental money, and for providing accurate accounts.

The Chief Finance and Information Officer is responsible for:

  • strategic leadership
  • financial management
  • development of the organisation’s information assets
  • ICT
  • business planning and legal functions

The Programme and Contract Assurance Director is responsible for:

  • contract assurance
  • programme office
  • engineering
  • procurement

The Innovation and Services Director is responsible for:

  • leading the adoption of mine water heat as a low carbon heat source across the coalfields of Great Britain
  • strategic development of all value generating reports, products and advisory services
  • strategic leadership of innovation and heat projects
  • customer strategy and insights

This role is responsible for:

  • technical and environment operations
  • environment delivery – coal
  • environment delivery – metals
  • environment programme office
  • resilience

The Operations and Sustainability Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the Coal Authority and its operational teams
  • building and maintaining strong relationships with key partners across England, Scotland and Wales to enable effective delivery of the Coal Authority’s work.
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence programme.
  • leadership and delivery of the Coal Authority Development functions which include licensing coal mining operations, permitting work that impacts coal and coal mine workings, planning statutory consultation and managing Coal Authority property.
  • leadership and delivery of the coal tip safety work in Wales and Coal Authority owned tips.
  • leadership and delivery of all aspects of the Coal Authority’s sustainability plan

This role is responsible for:

  • people and organisational development
  • health, safety, wellbeing and facilities
  • community response and stakeholder management
  • corporate and colleague engagement
  • CEO office

This role is responsible for:

  • leadership of the inland salinity programme
  • providing strategic input in to the Mining Remediation Authority’s executive leadership team, supporting the organisation’s delivery in line with its values

The Operations Director is responsible for:

  • strategy development and leadership for the organisation and its operational teams
  • building and maintaining strong relationships with key partners across England, Scotland and Wales to enable effective delivery of the organisation’s work
  • leadership and delivery of the public safety and subsidence programme
  • leadership and delivery of the organisation’s functions, which include licensing coal mining operations, permitting work that impacts coal and coal mine workings, planning statutory consultation and managing organisation’s property
  • leadership and delivery of the coal tip safety work in Wales and Mining Remediation Authority owned tips

This role is responsible for:

  • environment operations
  • environment delivery – coal
  • environment delivery – metals

This role is responsible for:

  • leadership of the inland salinity programme
  • providing strategic input in to the Mining Remediation Authority’s executive leadership team, supporting the organisation’s delivery in line with its values

This role is responsible for:

  • environment operations
  • environment delivery – coal
  • environment delivery – metals

This role is responsible for:

  • digital - including service and assurance, technical delivery, web and design, architecture
  • information and artificial intelligence - including data management, data improvement, geospatial technologies and mining information consultants

This role provides strategic leadership to the organisation and is accountable to Parliament and other stakeholders for the authority’s performance, ensuring its functions are carried out with efficiency, transparency and value for public money.
